findings.reachability: Any logged-in user with view_woocommerce_reports or export_woocommerce_orders capability
findings.justification: When $_REQUEST['tab'] is absent or not in $this->tabs, the code dispatches $ajax_handler->$method() (line 537) without ever calling check_admin_referer(); the nonce check at line 546 only runs in the tab-routed branch, so all globally-handled AJAX methods (WC_Order_Export_Ajax) execute with no CSRF protection.

findings.reachability: Any user who can access the WooCommerce orders list page (shop_manager or above by default, but filterable)
findings.justification: The bulk action handler that marks and unmarks orders as exported (lines 599-625) performs no current_user_can() check; WordPress's handle_bulk_actions filter does not enforce capability, so a lower-privileged user who can reach the orders list can toggle the export status of any order.

findings.reachability: Any authenticated user who triggers the bulk export action, but the resulting redirect URL is nonce-free and can be replayed by an unauthenticated party if the URL leaks
findings.justification: The redirect URL constructed at line 597 (admin-ajax.php?action=order_exporter&method=export_download_bulk_file&...&ids=...) contains no nonce parameter; if this URL is shared or logged, a third party can download the bulk export file without authentication because the target AJAX method is registered under wp_ajax_order_exporter and whether it enforces auth is outside this file, but the generated link itself carries no CSRF/auth token.
